name: xharvester
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``xharvester`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: sudo ./xharvester (Linux/macOS); python xharvester (Windows, as Admin); tsu ./xharvester (Termux)

examples.command: sudo ./xharvester (Linux/macOS); python xharvester (Windows, as Admin); tsu ./xharvester (Termux)

algorithms: WiFi interface discovery via iwconfig parsing
monitor-mode toggle via airmon-ng check kill + start
network scan via airodump-ng --output-format csv + CSV parse for BSSID/ESSID/channel/signal/encryption
client enumeration via airodump-ng --bssid --channel + station-CSV parse
handshake capture via airodump-ng --write pcap + aircrack-ng verification loop
evil-twin AP via hostapd conf broadcast
dictionary crack via aircrack-ng -w wordlist handshake.cap
produces: WPS PIN / WPA PSK strings parsed from reaver stdout
cracked PSK from aircrack-ng 'KEY FOUND!' line
novelty: none — thin wrapper. Every WiFi capability is a subprocess call to airodump-ng/aireplay-ng/aircrack-ng/reaver/hostapd that KFIOSA already uses. No PMKID (hcxdumptool/hcxpcapngtool never invoked), no wash/WPS scan (wps_enabled flag is never populated), no GPS/wardrive tagging, no probe-request sniffing, no hidden-SSID reveal (hidden SSIDs only relabeled "Hidden_<last5>" with no deauth+beacon reveal), no EAPOL monitor, no beacon/probe parsing beyond airodump CSV fields. The only added value is a TTY menu, cross-platform install scripts, and a text report generator.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
